title: Avoid Direct Logging to Standard Outputs in Java
description: In software development, logging is crucial for debugging. When logging, ensure that the logs are accessible, uniformly formatted, properly recorded, and securely logged when dealing with sensitive data. Standard outputs (e.g., System.out, System.err) do not meet these requirements. Use a dedicated logger instead.
recommendation: Replace standard outputs such as System.out or System.err with a dedicated logger for logging messages.
impact: Improper logging can lead to difficulty in debugging and potential security risks. Using a dedicated logger ensures uniformity, accessibility, safety, and proper recording of logs.

checks.message: Replace standard outputs such as System.out or System.err with a dedicated logger for logging messages.
